resources.string: PeekPreview shows a floating preview of a conversation when you long-press it in Google Messages, Facebook Messenger, or Instagram Direct, so you can read a message without opening the chat.\n\nTo do this, PeekPreview needs to read the on-screen text of the row you long-press. It only reads text in response to your long-press action — it does not record, log, or transmit your messages, and nothing leaves your device. You can turn it off any time from the app or from Accessibility settings.
Long-press a conversation to preview it, right from your messages list — without opening the chat.
Choose which apps peek is active in. The master switch above turns all of them on or off.
Preview quality may vary between apps — Google Messages tends to work most reliably; Messenger and Instagram previews depend on what each app exposes and may occasionally show partial or no text.
On long-press, dump the full accessibility tree under tag \"PeekPreview\" so you can tune extraction for Messenger/Instagram.
Grant the permissions below to enable Peek Preview.
